 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xo3e.3","title":"TEST: fcp.elasticsearch Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xo3e.4","title":"E2E: fcp.elasticsearch Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xpuo0","title":"Workspace-wide clippy sweep (fcp-registry, fcp-annas-archive, fcp-tailscale, fcp-sandbox)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:54:59.707974Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T22:55:08.966525Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T22:55:08.966513Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":847,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xpuo0","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Fixed workspace clippy -D warnings across 4 crates: fcp-registry (missing #[test] attrs, unit struct default(), assert_eq bool), fcp-annas-archive (redundant clones, empty String, Duration, literal separators), fcp-tailscale (redundant clone), fcp-sandbox (11 duplicate test names, 2 wildcard match arms). All 759 tests pass, workspace clippy fully clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:55:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-xson4","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-8: fcp-raptorq SymbolEnvelope round-trip + ZoneKeyIdMismatch fast-fail","description":"fcp-raptorq's SymbolEnvelope::encrypt/decrypt is the higher-level envelope (sibling of fcp-protocol's encrypt_symbol/decrypt_symbol that br-2qkn0 already pins). Its key differentiator is a ZoneKeyIdMismatch structured error returned BEFORE running the AEAD primitive when the provided zone_key_id does not match the envelope's stored zone_key_id. That fast-fail path is distinct from the AAD-authentication-failure path and zero conformance tests exist. Add session_raptorq_envelope_conformance.rs pinning round-trip (ChaCha+XChaCha), ZoneKeyIdMismatch fast-fail with correct expected/found, wrong zone_key (AAD fail), and binding to sender_instance_id/frame_seq/esi/source_id/epoch_id. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-raptorq).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:52:05.333685Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:52:09.152426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","raptorq","tests"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-xson4","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-8: fcp-raptorq SymbolEnvelope round-trip + ZoneKeyIdMismatch fast-fail","description":"fcp-raptorq's SymbolEnvelope::encrypt/decrypt is the higher-level envelope (sibling of fcp-protocol's encrypt_symbol/decrypt_symbol that br-2qkn0 already pins). Its key differentiator is a ZoneKeyIdMismatch structured error returned BEFORE running the AEAD primitive when the provided zone_key_id does not match the envelope's stored zone_key_id. That fast-fail path is distinct from the AAD-authentication-failure path and zero conformance tests exist. Add session_raptorq_envelope_conformance.rs pinning round-trip (ChaCha+XChaCha), ZoneKeyIdMismatch fast-fail with correct expected/found, wrong zone_key (AAD fail), and binding to sender_instance_id/frame_seq/esi/source_id/epoch_id. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-raptorq).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:52:05.333685Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:53:45.241600Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:53:45.241113Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","raptorq","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3015,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xson4","author":"jemanuel","text":"94d30b20 adds raptorq_envelope_conformance.rs with 13 tests pinning fcp-raptorq's higher-level SymbolEnvelope: ChaCha+XChaCha round-trips, ZoneKeyIdMismatch structured fast-fail (with expected/found payload), wrong-key DecryptFailed (separate from mismatch), ciphertext+tag tamper, AAD binding to object_id/epoch_id/source_id/sender_instance_id, nonce binding to frame_seq+esi, and algorithm cross-rejection. All 13 pass in <0.01s. Uses only public fcp_raptorq API. Distinct from br-2qkn0 (fcp-protocol's lower-level envelope).","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:53:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xukzs","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-1: stateless HelloRetry cookie compute/verify transcript binding","description":"fcp-protocol's compute_cookie + verify_cookie are the stateless anti-DoS MAC used for the HelloRetry cookie challenge. They have inline coverage in session.rs but ZERO cross-crate conformance tests in fcp-conformance/tests/. Add session_hello_retry_cookie_conformance.rs pinning: (1) round-trip compute_cookie -> verify_cookie passes, (2) wrong cookie_key rejected, (3) transcript binding to hello.from / hello.eph_pubkey / hello.nonce / hello.timestamp / hello.suites — each tampered field MUST cause verify_cookie to return InvalidCookie. Small, focused; cap each test at sub-second. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-protocol).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:22:19.147902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:23:29.381708Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:23:29.381223Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2992,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xukzs","author":"jemanuel","text":"c2e3f66e adds session_hello_retry_cookie_conformance.rs with 10 tests pinning compute_cookie/verify_cookie: round-trip, wrong-key rejection, single-byte tamper, transcript binding to hello.{from,to,eph_pubkey,nonce,timestamp,suites}, and determinism. All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test session_hello_retry_cookie_conformance. Uses only public fcp_protocol::session API.","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:23:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","title":"[MOR/C1.6] Revocation timing integration tests proving bounded enforcement window","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:31:05.062285Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:49.530151Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:49.529475Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["revocation","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-4gbac","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:48.829954Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:45.089893Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-800e3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:49.570897Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8s56e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:48.085469Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:44.062942Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2632,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Create a revocation timing integration test suite in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/ or crates/fcp-conformance/tests/ that proves revocation enforcement within a bounded time window. Tests should simulate: (1) issue capability, use it, revoke it, verify rejection; (2) concurrent revocation and use (TOCTOU test with seal); (3) revocation propagation across simulated 3-node mesh; (4) degraded mode: revocation with stale cache; (5) Critical/Risky/Safe freshness class enforcement. WHY: Multiple modes identified that revocation is tested for correctness (hash links, signatures) but NOT for timing. The gap is: revocation WORKS but we do not know HOW FAST it works or whether the timing guarantees hold under load. WHERE: New test file(s) in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/revocation_timing.rs or crates/fcp-conformance/tests/revocation_timing.rs. Uses existing test infrastructure (fcp-testkit mock servers, wiremock). ACCEPTANCE: (1) At least 8 integration tests covering all scenarios above. (2) Tests pass with bounded assertion: revocation enforcement within N seconds (configurable, default 5s for 3-node mesh). (3) TOCTOU test: issue seal, insert revocation concurrently, verify seal re-validation catches it. (4) Degraded mode test: simulate partition, verify Critical ops abort, Safe ops warn. (5) All tests pass in CI. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 3-4 days. DEPENDS ON: C1.1 (seal pattern), C1.2 (exact membership), C1.3 (freshness class) — tests exercise all three mechanisms.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:32:30Z"},{"id":2650,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C3.1 (phantom tokens) because the revocation timing tests must exercise the seal pattern, which is part of the phantom token verification flow. Tests should verify that unverified tokens cannot be used even when revocation checks would pass.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:44Z"},{"id":2675,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-xy1e4","author":"jemanuel","text":"C1.6 done: 10 integration tests in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/revocation_timing.rs. Scenarios: issue-use-revoke-reject, TOCTOU seal, 3-node mesh propagation, degraded-mode SLA, freshness class policy, priority push roundtrip, 10K zero false positives, bounded enforcement, SLA boundary, priority interval. Clippy clean. Commit 11bacac5.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xyyt","title":"[IDEA-03] Event Ordering Metadata + Per-Key Sequentialization","description":"# Event Ordering Metadata + Per-Key Sequentialization

## Goal
Expose explicit ordering metadata for events and provide SDK utilities for per-key sequential processing (per chat/thread/channel) to prevent reordering bugs.

## Background / Why
Discord relies on gateway sequence ordering; Telegram relies on per-chat sequentialization. Today, these ordering contracts are implicit. Making them explicit improves correctness and cross-connector consistency.

## Scope
- Extend event metadata to include ordering fields (`stream_key`, `sequence`, `ordering_policy`).
- Provide SDK helper for per-key sequential processing with bounded queues.

## Deliverables
- Event metadata schema update + documentation.
- `SequentialEventProcessor` utility with backpressure and timeout limits.
- Example usage in at least one streaming and one polling connector.

## Tests
- Unit tests for ordering semantics and queue bounds.
- Integration test with mixed keys to verify isolation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- EventEnvelope ordering fields are present and documented.
- Connectors can opt-in to sequentialization with minimal boilerplate.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

EventEnvelope includes topic + sequence. Extensive test coverage. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:20:10Z"}]}
